name: documents
notes: A single row is inserted per successful upload: name comes from the 'name' form field (falls back to the original filename), file_type is the MIME type validated by multer, file_path is '/uploads/{uuid}{ext}', tags is always NULL from this component (UploadButton does not submit a tags field), and uploaded_by is the authenticated user's email or NULL for unauthenticated uploads.

After the INSERT, a database trigger (trg_sync_to_v2) automatically copies the new row to documents_v2 but deliberately omits the tags column — this is a known bug in the legacy schema (see migration 003-create-trigger.sql).

name: documents_v2
notes: Written exclusively by the sync_document_to_v2 trigger, not by application code. This table receives every column from the documents insert EXCEPT tags — the omission is intentional in the legacy code and is a documented bug.
The Java/Angular reimplementation should treat documents_v2 as the authoritative table and ensure tags are persisted there directly, eliminating the trigger entirely.
